content.text:
[cron:feee69f8-6016-4f8f-a09c-08b39d9a9191 github-backup] Run these commands: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push). Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.

content.thinking:
The git backup completed successfully - 2 files changed with 108 insertions. Since something was committed and pushed, but the instruction says to report only if there was an error, and nothing changed (the instruction was "nothing to commit" which didn't happen - there was a commit), I should check if there's actually anything to report.

Wait, re-reading the instruction: "Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error."

The backup succeeded - it committed and pushed. So no error, no message needed.

Also the final part: "If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY."

But something DID change - there was a commit with 2 files. But that's not "nothing to commit" - that's the successful case.

The instruction says report only if error. No error occurred, so NO_REPLY.
